headline: The placebo that worked even when people knew
subtext: Honest sugar pills eased IBS, showing how powerfully the brain shapes the gut.
caption:
Here is something that should not work, but did. Patients were given a placebo and told plainly it was a placebo. Their symptoms still improved.

In a Harvard trial, people with irritable bowel syndrome who took an openly labelled sugar pill reported greater relief than those given nothing. The ritual of treatment, and the expectation of care, changed how the gut felt.

This is the brain-body link in plain sight. The same wiring shapes chronic pain, fibromyalgia, tinnitus and long COVID, where symptoms are real and strongly influenced by a sensitised nervous system.

It does not mean it is all in your head. It means the brain is part of the cure. Start at https://neurowaze.health/start-my-recovery

#IBS #ChronicPain #Neuroplasticity #Fibromyalgia #Tinnitus #LongCovid #MindBody #PainScience #PainRecovery #Neurowaze
first_comment: Kaptchuk et al. (2010), Placebos without Deception: A Randomized Controlled Trial in Irritable Bowel Syndrome, PLoS ONE, 5(12), e15591. Open-label placebos produced significantly greater symptom relief than no treatment. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0015591
